pending.body: `fix-now` High: exhausted per-member retries still bubble up as retryable stage failures. In [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:172) and [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2390), `rewrite_transient_retry_exhaustion_error` preserves the original `FailureClass`, and `invoke_final_review_member` returns that retryable `TransportFailure`/`Timeout` after the member has already exhausted its own retry budget. The engine then sees that class in [src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5844) and retries the entire `final_review` stage again. That turns a supposed per-invocation retry into compounded stage-level retries, multiplying backend calls and backoff delays. The changed resume test at [tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:1227) now takes about 72s for exactly this reason. This violates the bead’s scope guard (`per-invocation transient retry, not per-stage restart`) and means an exhausted reviewer/arbiter failure is not actually terminal for the stage.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
